best_law.note: R^2 and MSE here are computed on surrogate probability outputs, NOT on binary labels. See 'classification_metrics' for held-out classification performance.

binomial_test_H0_p90.interpretation: NOT FALSIFIED: cannot reject H0: p >= 90%

binomial_test_H0_p80.interpretation: NOT FALSIFIED: cannot reject H0: p >= 80%

sequential_testing.replication_protocol: Reserve an independent replication holdout of ≥500 systems (separate seed, never used in any search/tuning). Apply the final promoted law to this holdout ONCE at α=0.0019 (Bonferroni for 26 tests). Report this as the primary validation result.

replication_holdout.verdict: PROMOTED — law passes replication at corrected α

speedup_accounting.measurement_method: wall-clock timing on CPU (single-threaded N-body vs surrogate forward pass)
speedup_accounting.note: Inference speedup measures only the forward-pass cost. Amortised speedup includes the full pipeline (data generation, surrogate training, symbolic search) spread over all queries. N-body baseline is a simple symplectic integrator; production codes would be faster.

runtime_detail.note: Inference speedup measures only the surrogate forward-pass cost. Amortised speedup spreads the full pipeline (data generation, surrogate training, symbolic search, evaluation) over all inference queries. At production scale (>10k queries), amortised speedup converges to inference speedup.

conservative_conclusion:
CONSERVATIVE CONCLUSION
============================================================

Replication holdout (n=500, independent seed): accuracy 97.8% (95% Wilson CI [96.1%, 98.8%]). Verdict: PROMOTED — law passes replication at corrected α.

Multi-context controlled ΔHill sweeps yield pooled exponent 1.94 (95% CI [1.57, 2.30]). Exponent=3 is NOT within the pooled 95% CI.
  low_25pct: exponent=2.03 (CI [1.51, 2.55])
  median_50pct: exponent=0.90 (CI [0.48, 1.41])
  high_75pct: exponent=4.62 (CI [3.66, 5.49])

Adversarial retraining: added 100 boundary + 27 adversarial samples. Probe accuracy 94.6% → 98.8% (Δ=+4.2pp).
  near_boundary_2.5-4.5: 88.1% → 97.2% (Δ=+9.2pp)
  far_stable_>4.5: 99.1% → 100.0% (Δ=+0.9pp)
  far_unstable_<2.5: 100.0% → 100.0% (Δ=+0.0pp)

Sequential testing: 26 implicit tests, Bonferroni α=0.0019.

ASSESSMENT:
The candidate law '0.007772 * delta_Hill_01^3' passes the one-time replication holdout at corrected α. It may be presented as an empirically validated proxy — NOT a physical law — within the stated regime (2-planet, low-e, low-mass).

The cubic exponent should be presented as a PARSIMONIOUS SURROGATE supported by some evidence but not yet proven as the mechanistic scaling. AIC analysis shows the data does not strongly distinguish free vs fixed exponents. Independent replication with larger samples and broader architectures is needed before any physical interpretation is warranted.

checks.name: train_test_overlap
checks.detail: 0 exact duplicate rows shared between train and test.

checks.name: target_correlation
checks.detail: No features suspiciously correlated with labels.

checks.name: test_extrapolation
checks.detail: 6 features have test values outside training range: ['e_0', 'inc_0', 'mu_1', 'e_1', 'delta_Hill_01', 'nearest_mmr_01']

checks.name: feature_construction
checks.detail: Features are constructed from orbital elements (a, e, inc, Omega, omega, M) and planet masses BEFORE integration or labeling. Labels are assigned by the Hill stability criterion (analytical), which depends on the same orbital elements but through a different function (separation in mutual Hill radii vs critical threshold). No integration outcome is used in feature computation.

dimensional_consistency.note: All features are ratios or angles (dimensionless by construction). Numerical coefficient absorbs any remaining scale.

identifiability.statement: NOT SATISFIED: bootstrap coefficient stds (0.3427) are 51.6x larger than theoretical 0.0066. Exponent estimates are unreliable. Causes: model misspecification, collinear features, or insufficient data (n=227).

generalisation.statement: With probability >= 95%, true risk R(h) <= 0.6168 for complexity <= 3, n = 227. The bound 0.6168 >= 0.5 is VACUOUS at this sample size. More data or lower-complexity expressions needed.

prediction.prediction: The symbolic search produced candidate law '0.007772 * delta_Hill_01^3'. On the current held-out set (n=40) the law yields 95.0% accuracy (95% Wilson CI: [83.5%, 98.6%]) and an apparent ~1x speedup versus direct N-body integration. Bootstrap and targeted diagnostics show exponent estimates are not yet stable and some fresh samples are out-of-distribution relative to training. Therefore these results are PRELIMINARY. We will consider the law empirically validated only after (i) a targeted ΔHill identifiability experiment confirms the exponent within ±0.2 at 95% confidence, (ii) replication on an independent holdout of ≥500 systems yields accuracy ≥90% (one-sided binomial test at α=0.0250 after sequential correction), and (iii) counterexample search fails to find regimes where accuracy falls below 70%.
prediction.caveats: Small held-out set (n=40); results are preliminary and must be validated on >= 500 fresh systems. Bonferroni-corrected alpha = 0.0250 (for 2 iterative tests). Sequential testing plan in effect: the iterative discovery process and counterexample searches create many implicit tests beyond the nominal Bonferroni correction above.
prediction.falsification_criteria: This prediction is falsified if, on an independent replication holdout of >= 500 2-planet systems drawn uniformly from the stated regime (separate seed, never used in search/tuning), accuracy < 90% (one-sided binomial test at Bonferroni-corrected alpha = 0.0250 for 2 tests: reject H0: p >= 80% if p-value < 0.0250), OR a targeted ΔHill identifiability experiment yields exponent outside ±0.2 of 3 at 95% confidence, OR the discovered expression fails dimensional analysis, OR the counterexample search identifies a regime where accuracy drops below 70%.
